stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking activity around a significant open-source LLM.

stories.title: South Korea to spend $1T on more memory chip production and humanoid robots
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: South Korea is committing massive investment ($1T) into memory chip production.
The national strategy aims to achieve a leadership position in physical AI.
A key goal is the commercial deployment of humanoid robots by 2028.

stories.title: Google warns EU's plans to weaken its monopoly could expose user data
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is warning the EU that its proposed regulations, which aim to curb its monopoly, could compromise user privacy.
The EU's plan mandates Google to share search data with competitors and open up AI features on Android.
The core conflict is between the EU's antitrust goals and Google's concerns over potential data exposure risks.

stories.title: Manufactured Confidence: How Memory Consolidation Turns Hearsay into Confident Facts
stories.author: Alex Kwon
bullet_points: LLM agents use compressed memory products (like mem0, LangMem) to store conversation history as 'facts'.
The research demonstrates that this memory rewriting process can manufacture false confidence in the agent.
A casual, unverified statement can be transformed into a confident, obeyed assertion without external attack.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML frameworks like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its application in large language model tasks.
The associated tags confirm its relevance within the transformer and Llama ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in faster response times and lower memory usage for models.
The updated engine also leads to higher quality outputs from the language models.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Introduction of NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra, a new AI model.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM deployment.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads on Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and adoption.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is connecting messaging workflows directly to local, private AI capabilities.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its zero-configuration approach, eliminating the need for environment variables or config files.

stories.title: WhatsApp is launching usernames: here’s how to reserve yours
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: WhatsApp is implementing usernames, allowing users to communicate without sharing their phone numbers.
The feature aims to enhance user privacy by concealing phone numbers from non-contacts.
The rollout is scheduled for 'later this year'.

stories.title: Lawmakers want to ban AI companies from selling your health data
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Lawmakers are proposing a ban on selling Americans' health and location data to data brokers.
The proposal specifically targets data shared with AI chatbots like ChatGPT and Claude.
Senator Elizabeth Warren and Representative Mary Gay Scanlon are driving the new legislation.

stories.title: US offers $10 million for info on group behind Signal and WhatsApp hacking spree
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: US government is offering a $10 million reward for intelligence on the hacking group targeting Signal and WhatsApp.
The cyber operation is attributed to two distinct Russia-state-sponsored groups.
The hacking activity has reportedly been active since at least March.

stories.title: Vision-Default, Prior-Override: Causal Mechanisms of Perception-Knowledge Conflict in Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Michal Golovanevsky
bullet_points: Investigates the causal mechanisms by which Vision-Language Models (VLMs) resolve conflicts between visual input and stored world knowledge.
Uses advanced mechanistic interpretability techniques, including activation patching across different model components (residual stream, attention heads, MLP sublayers).
Provides a component-level causal account of perception-knowledge conflict, addressing a gap in prior behavioral studies of multimodal systems.

stories.title: Mechanism-Driven Monitors for Preemptive Detection of LLM Training Instability
stories.author: Yang Zheng
bullet_points: Addresses a critical, costly problem in frontier LLM training: detecting instability before catastrophic failure.
Proposes a novel solution using 'mechanism-driven monitors' derived from the functional role of model modules.
Focuses on preemptive detection, overcoming the issue where standard metrics (loss/gradient norms) remain normal during instability.

stories.title: LongCat-2.0, a large-scale MoE model with 1.6T total and 48B Active
stories.author: benjiro29
bullet_points: Introduction of LongCat-2.0, a new large-scale Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model.
The model boasts a massive total parameter count of 1.6 Trillion.
It features a smaller, more efficient active parameter count of 48 Billion.

stories.title: Ornith-1.0: self-improving open-source models for agentic coding
stories.author: danboarder
bullet_points: Introduction of Ornith-1.0, a new open-source model focused on agentic coding.
The model features self-improving capabilities, suggesting advanced autonomous development potential.
The open-source nature positions it as a significant contribution to the AI/ML community for practical application.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for subagents within Claude Code.
The new feature set includes the capability for web search functionality in Claude Code.
This indicates an expansion of Ollama's capabilities to support more complex, multi-step AI workflows.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to support local image generation.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Tidal won’t pay royalties on AI-generated music but isn’t banning it outright
stories.author: Andrew Liszewski
bullet_points: Tidal is implementing new policies regarding AI-generated music rather than an outright ban.
Starting July 15th, 100% AI-generated tracks will be labeled with an icon to inform listeners.
Currently, AI-generated tracks on Tidal are not monetizable, aligning with efforts to 'protect artists'.

stories.title: Sony’s next-gen PlayStation will go ‘beyond the living room’
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Sony is planning a next-generation PlayStation that aims to expand gameplay beyond the traditional living room setting.
The development is partly a strategic response to users migrating from console gaming to PC gaming.
The announcement was made during a Q&A session with investors, indicating a high-level strategic focus.

stories.title: US renewable boom passes key milestone in April
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: US renewable energy generation has reached a significant milestone.
Small-scale solar power played a crucial role in this growth.
Renewables now generate nearly three times the power of coal in the US.

stories.title: Supreme Court ruling guts government’s use of geofence warrants
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The Supreme Court addressed the constitutionality of government use of geofence warrants.
The ruling did not definitively strike down the use of geofence warrants by the government.
The decision represents a significant legal development concerning digital surveillance and privacy.

stories.title: Adaptive Financial Transformer with Regime-Gated Attention for Stock Return Prediction
stories.author: Dishan Sarkar
bullet_points: Proposes Adaptive Financial Transformer (AFT) to predict stock returns in non-stationary markets.
AFT uses a Market Regime Encoder and Adaptive Gate Network to dynamically bias self-attention.
The model groups 95 engineered financial features into 11 semantic categories for nuanced analysis.

stories.title: PHF: Privileged Hidden Flow for On-Policy Self-Distillation
stories.author: Ying Sun
bullet_points: Introduces PHF (Privileged Hidden Flow) to enhance On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD).
PHF addresses a limitation in existing OPSD methods by going beyond output distribution matching.
It specifically distills the internal computation of a privileged teacher model, not just the final output.

stories.title: Covering the Unseen: Information Demand Coverage Optimization for Retrieval-Augmented Generation
stories.author: Feida Zhu
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) where simple top-k context selection fails for complex queries.
Introduces GeoRAG, a novel approach that reframes context selection as Information Demand Coverage Optimization.
GeoRAG generates a multi-dimensional demand distribution using diverse sub-query generation to improve context relevance.

stories.title: Hierarchical Experimentalist Agents
stories.author: Scott Niekum
bullet_points: Current LLM agents are limited by relying on fixed, parametric knowledge, retrieval, or search.
This paradigm fails when dealing with novel domains or complex queries requiring real-world interaction.
The research proposes 'Hierarchical Experimentalist Agents' to overcome the limitations of prior knowledge alone.

stories.title: Deterministic Decisions for High-Stakes AI. A Zero-Egress Pipeline with the Deployability of RAG and the Accuracy of Machine Learning
stories.author: Craig Atkinson
bullet_points: Identifies 'intervention bias' as a novel failure mode in zero-shot LLM educational advisory agents.
Demonstrates that without task-specific training, LLMs can recommend actions contrary to an optimal oracle policy.
Proposes a 'Zero-Egress Pipeline' approach to improve the deployability and accuracy of high-stakes AI systems like RAG.

stories.title: The Complexity Ceiling Benchmark: A Multi-Domain Evaluation of Sequential Reasoning Under Depth Scaling
stories.author: Yash Sinha
bullet_points: Introduces the Complexity Ceiling Benchmark (CCB) to test language model reasoning decay.
CCB systematically varies task depth (N=5 to 50) across three distinct reasoning regimes.
Findings reveal consistent patterns of performance degradation across frontier and open-weight LLMs as complexity increases.

stories.title: Adaptive Financial Transformer with Regime-Gated Attention for Stock Return Prediction
stories.author: Dishan Sarkar
bullet_points: Proposes Adaptive Financial Transformer (AFT) to predict stock returns in non-stationary markets.
Incorporates a Market Regime Encoder and Adaptive Gate Network to dynamically bias self-attention.
Differentiates from standard Transformers by grouping 95 financial features into 11 semantic categories.

stories.title: Reliability, Faithfulness, and the Limits of Post-hoc Explanations of Opaque Scientific Models
stories.author: Helen Jin
bullet_points: The paper critiques the reliance on post-hoc explanation methods for interpreting complex scientific ML models.
It distinguishes between model reliability (matching phenomenon outcomes) and explanation faithfulness (matching the model's internal logic).
The core argument is that neither reliability nor faithfulness guarantees that the explanation provides true insight into the underlying scientific phenomenon.

stories.title: Two kinds of robustness are not the same: disentangling fault tolerance and low-SNR robustness in multi-domain event detection on real data
stories.author: Isao Kurosawa
bullet_points: The paper distinguishes between two types of robustness in event detection: fault tolerance (sensor failure) and low-SNR robustness (noise interference).
This distinction is crucial for real-world applications like induced-seismicity monitoring for CCS and geothermal operations.
The research addresses the common conflation of these two failure modes, suggesting architectural complexity might be misattributed as robustness.

stories.title: SP-CACW: Convergence-Aware Client Weighting for Selfish Personalized Learning
stories.author: Kfir Y. Levy
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of standard federated learning where global optimization harms clients with unique data distributions.
Introduces 'selfish personalization,' allowing a target client to optimize its own risk using peer gradients.
Proposes SP-CACW, a convergence-aware client-weighting framework to manage aggregation effectively.

stories.title: Hierarchical Experimentalist Agents
stories.author: Scott Niekum
bullet_points: Current LLM agents are limited because they primarily rely on fixed, parametric knowledge or retrieval methods.
This reliance fails when dealing with novel domains or complex, long-horizon tasks requiring real-world interaction.
The research proposes 'Hierarchical Experimentalist Agents' to overcome the limitations of purely data-driven LLMs in complex physical systems.

stories.title: Beyond Trajectory Matching: Reflow with Marginal Distribution Alignment
stories.author: Ke Deng
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational inefficiency of high-quality generative models (like diffusion models) which rely on solving learned Ordinary Differential Equations (ODEs).
It proposes 'reflow-based distillation' as an acceleration strategy to simplify complex teacher ODE trajectories.
The goal is to enable student models to approximate the teacher's transport dynamics using significantly fewer sampling steps.

stories.title: Towards Automating Scientific Review with Google's Paper Assistant Tool
stories.author: Vincent Cohen-Addad
bullet_points: The paper addresses the scalability crisis in traditional human peer review due to the rapid acceleration of AI-assisted scientific discovery.
It proposes deploying AI tools, specifically mentioning Google's Paper Assistant, to automate and accelerate the scientific verification and review process.
The research introduces a taxonomy to frame the necessary transition toward AI-driven scientific validation.

stories.title: HPRO: Hierarchical Progressive Reward Optimization via Preference Extraction for Emotional Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Xiangmin Xu
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in current LLM-based Text-to-Speech (TTS) models: the tendency to produce statistically averaged prosody, lacking emotional expressiveness.
Proposes 'HPRO,' a Hierarchical Progressive Reward Optimization method utilizing preference extraction to improve emotional TTS.
Identifies and aims to solve structural mismatches in preference-driven optimization, specifically 'information conflict' between content and emotion gradients.

stories.title: Cognitive Episodes in LLM Reasoning Traces Enable Interpretable Human Item Difficulty Prediction
stories.author: Dawei Zhou
bullet_points: The research proposes a novel approach to predicting human item difficulty in educational assessments.
It shifts the focus from item text alone to observable cognitive processes within Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning traces.
This method aims to provide a more interpretable and less resource-intensive alternative to traditional human calibration methods.

stories.title: From Tokens to States: LLMs as a Special Case of World Models and the Continuous Path Beyond
stories.author: Paul Dubois
bullet_points: The paper challenges the binary view separating LLMs (token predictors) and World Models (reality simulators).
It posits that LLMs can be viewed as a degenerate special case of World Models, where the state space is defined by token sequences.
The work aligns with arguments (like LeCun's) suggesting that achieving AGI may require moving beyond purely autoregressive token prediction toward latent-space architectures.

stories.title: MultiHashFormer: Hash-based Generative Language Models
stories.author: Nikolaos Aletras
bullet_points: Introduces MultiHashFormer, a new framework for hash-based generative language models.
Addresses the limitation of prior hashing methods (many-to-one collisions) that prevented use in causal LMs.
Allows for token representation using unique hash signatures to maintain parameter efficiency in autoregressive models.

stories.title: Can LLMs Judge Better Than They Generate? Evaluating Task Asymmetry, Mechanistic Interpretability and Transferability for In-Context QA
stories.author: Sambaran Bandyopadhyay
bullet_points: Challenges the common assumption that LLM evaluation (LLM-as-a-Judge) is inherently easier than generation.
Conducts controlled in-context QA testing to isolate evaluation performance from general parametric knowledge.
Findings show that generation accuracy surpassed self-evaluation accuracy on three out of four tested benchmarks.
